{"query":"Julian Assange charter flight VJT199 cost crowdfunding campaign total raised Saipan Canberra","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1828,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/julian-assange-arrives-home-australia-free-man-us-plea-deal-rcna158970","title":"Julian Assange returns home to Australia a free man after U.S. plea deal","content":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange returned home to Australia a free man Wednesday after pleading guilty in a deal that ended his yearslong legal battle over the publication of U.S. military secrets.
Tonight that has happened,” Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese told reporters shortly after Assange’s arrival.
A crowdfunding campaign launched by his supporters had raised almost $400,000 as of Wednesday morning.
With the help of whistleblower Chelsea Manning, the website leaked about 250,000 State Department diplomatic cables, as well as classified U.S. military documents and videos from the U.S. wars in Iraq and Afghanistan, including video of an Apache helicopter attack in Baghdad that killed civilians."},{"url":"https://crowdsourcingweek.com/blog/crowdfunding-julian-assange/","title":"Crowdfunding the Cost of Julian Assange’s Astonishing Flight Home","content":"He's a free man at last, but at the cost of a $520,000 debt to the Australian Government.
Australian journalist and founder of WikiLeaks, Julian Assange, is at last a free man.
And Julian Assange’s flight home to Australia to reunite with his family, via a court hearing in Saipan, has put him US$520,000 in debt to the Australian Government.
At the time of writing, the total raised had reached 82% of the target, with backing from over 9,000 donors.
Though there have been questions as to why the crowdfunding target is £520,000 to pay a debt of US$520,000."},{"url":"https://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/revealed-julian-assanges-780-000-flight-home-after-london-jail-release/x17lvthsg","title":"Julian Assange's $780,000 flight home after jail release | SBS News","content":"# Revealed: Julian Assange's $780,000 flight home after London jail release
- It has been revealed his trip back to Australia cost more than $781,480.
- A foundation has agreed to pay most of the costs, but Australian taxpayers will still face a bill of about $100,000.
The charter flight cost $781,480, according to documents tabled in the Senate.
That expense will be reimbursed by the Germany-based Wau Holland Foundation, but about $100,000 will be charged to the public purse for travel expenses for Australia's United States Ambassador, Kevin Rudd, UK High Commissioner Stephen Smith, and "other associated Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade costs"."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/media/article/2024/jun/25/julian-assange-australia-reaction-wikileaks-founder-plea-deal","title":"Australia’s most senior diplomat in UK flying with Julian Assange to Saipan | Julian Assange | The Guardian","content":"The high commissioner to the UK, Stephen Smith, had travelled with Assange out of the country, Albanese said, adding that Australia’s ambassador to the US, Kevin Rudd, was “also providing important assistance”.
The Labor MP Julian Hill said: “No one should judge Julian for accepting a deal to get the hell out of there and come home."},{"url":"https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2024-06-26/wikileaks-founder-julian-assange-returns-to-australia-a-free-man-after-us-legal-battle-ends","title":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange returns to Australia a free man - Los Angeles Times","content":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange gestures after landing in Canberra, Australia, on Wednesday.
Assange was accused of receiving and publishing hundreds of thousands of war logs and diplomatic cables that included details of U.S. military wrongdoing in Iraq and Afghanistan.
Albanese told Parliament that Assange’s freedom, after he spent five years in a British prison fighting extradition to the United States, was the result of his government’s “careful, patient and determined work.”
Rycroft reported from Canberra and McGuirk from Melbourne."}]}
